pipeline_models._rationale: Cross-model critique. The drafter (Claude Sonnet) and the critic (GPT-5.5) are different models from different vendors. When they agree something is wrong, the signal is much stronger than one model self-critiquing. The decider (Claude Sonnet again) synthesizes the two perspectives. Same pattern gstack uses for /codex review of Claude-authored work.

series_anchor.label: Charles Comte — Traité de Législation (Volumes I-IV)
series_anchor.palette: lead grey, ivory, ink black, oxidized brass, considered neutrals
series_anchor.mood: 18th-century European salon at lamplight. Restrained, rational. The philosopher's writing desk after the household has gone to bed.
series_anchor.watercolor_treatment: Cool dry washes with controlled pigment. Allow paper grain to dominate in the negative space. A single warm note (oxidized brass or candle warmth) introduced sparingly to break the cool palette.

output.reasoning: With no excerpt available, the subject must carry the full symbolic weight of Comte's legislative theory — rational, systematic, Enlightenment jurisprudence. A single quill resting in a dry inkwell evokes the moment after argument is committed to page: law as written reason, the instrument of codification set down and still. It fits the series mood of a philosopher's desk after midnight.
output.subject: A single quill pen resting across the mouth of an empty inkwell
output.palette: Payne's grey, ivory (paper reserve), oxidized brass
output.composition: Quill and inkwell placed in the lower-left third of the composition, angling diagonally toward the upper right, the feather's tip dissolving into unpainted cream paper. Upper two-thirds is pure breathing room, paper grain visible throughout. The inkwell anchors the visual weight; the quill's vane fades before it reaches the edge.
output.watercolor_treatment: Cool wet-on-wet wash of Payne's grey laid first, pooling darkest at the inkwell's base and diluting to near-nothing along the quill shaft. Paper grain shows through the thin mid-tones. A single restrained glaze of oxidized brass on the inkwell's rim, applied dry-brush so the bristle marks are legible. Pencil under-drawing faintly visible at the inkwell's curved edge. No second wash on the feather — one dilute layer only, edges soft and bleeding.
output.prompt: A single quill pen rests diagonally across the mouth of a small empty inkwell, placed in the lower-left third of warm cream cotton paper. The inkwell is rendered in Payne's grey, pigment pooling darkest at its base in a wet-on-wet wash that dilutes upward until it nearly vanishes. One dry-brush glaze of oxidized brass catches the rim, bristle marks faintly legible in the thin pigment. The quill's shaft receives a single dilute Payne's grey wash, feathered edges bleeding softly into unpainted paper; the vane dissolves before reaching the edge. Pencil under-drawing ghosts along the inkwell's curve. More than two-thirds of the composition is untouched paper, cold-press grain dominating the negative space. No hard outlines, no second layer on the feather, no warmth except the single brass accent.

output.reasoning: Dominant failures are structural: the draft violates the one-object constraint and lets warmth spread into the paper field instead of reserving it for the single brass accent. With two high-severity issues, gate logic requires regeneration.

issues.problematic_phrase: A single quill pen rests diagonally across the mouth of a small empty inkwell
issues.suggestion: A single small empty inkwell rests in the lower-left third; remove the quill, feather, shaft, and vane details.

output.reasoning: Issue 0 is high severity and valid: the prompt describes two objects (quill pen AND inkwell) with extensive feather/shaft/vane detail, violating a single-object constraint implied by the series anchor's restrained focus. The critic is correct to strip the quill and center the composition on the inkwell alone. Issue 1 is high severity and correct: 'warm cream' drifts the paper tone toward warmth, conflicting with the series anchor's cool palette directive that reserves warmth only for the single brass accent. 'Ivory' is the anchor-specified neutral and should replace it. Both issues are accepted. The remaining prompt structure (wet-on-wet wash, brass rim accent, pencil under-drawing, negative space dominance, no hard outlines) is sound and should be preserved.

decisions.reason: Two objects (quill and inkwell) violate the single-subject restraint; removing the quill and all feather/shaft/vane details focuses the composition correctly on the inkwell alone.

'Warm cream' introduces warmth into the paper field, conflicting with the series anchor's directive to reserve the single warm note exclusively for the oxidized brass accent; 'ivory' is the anchor-specified neutral.

output.final_prompt: A single small empty inkwell rests in the lower-left third of ivory cotton paper. The inkwell is rendered in Payne's grey, pigment pooling darkest at its base in a wet-on-wet wash that dilutes upward until it nearly vanishes. One dry-brush glaze of oxidized brass catches the rim, bristle marks faintly legible in the thin pigment. Pencil under-drawing ghosts along the inkwell's curve. More than two-thirds of the composition is untouched paper, cold-press grain dominating the negative space. No hard outlines, no second layer on the inkwell body, no warmth except the single brass accent.
output.changes_summary: The quill pen and all associated feather, shaft, and vane details were removed so the inkwell stands as the sole subject; 'warm cream' was replaced with 'ivory' to keep the paper field cool and reserve warmth exclusively for the brass rim accent.

final_prompt: A single small empty inkwell rests in the lower-left third of ivory cotton paper. The inkwell is rendered in Payne's grey, pigment pooling darkest at its base in a wet-on-wet wash that dilutes upward until it nearly vanishes. One dry-brush glaze of oxidized brass catches the rim, bristle marks faintly legible in the thin pigment. Pencil under-drawing ghosts along the inkwell's curve. More than two-thirds of the composition is untouched paper, cold-press grain dominating the negative space. No hard outlines, no second layer on the inkwell body, no warmth except the single brass accent.
